last_completed_round.direction_name: Rewrite active PTX hot-band B delivery at the consumer boundary without CTA repack
last_completed_round.hypothesis: The restored `26.093568 ms` PTX hot-band branch proves that the active `128x128 K16` surface is now the right place to keep pushing, but the branch is still far from the `20 ms` target: tensor-active is only `48.43%`, barrier is `8.05%`, and `mio_throttle` remains `4.45%` while DRAM (`30.93%`) and L2 (`25.77%`) are not saturated. The best next move is to keep the existing 16-byte `cp.async` producer path and double-buffered shared tiles intact, then rewrite only the shared-to-register B consumer path inside the active PTX microkernel: warp-local permuted or XOR swizzle, explicit `ldmatrix` or lane-remap delivery, and tighter mirrored fragment reuse so operand delivery happens at the warp boundary instead of through any new CTA-level repack. This directly accepts the human-idea families `Bank Conflict`, `Register Reuse`, and `Ps2r` on the real active path.
